questions.question: How many organs are annotated in the MICA dataset?
evidence_snippets: Each scan includes annotations for 11 organs: brain, lungs, left ventricle of the heart, heart, small intestine, kidneys, liver, spleen, large intestine, right inguinal lymph node, and bone marrow.

questions.question: What software was used to annotate the organs in the CT scans?
evidence_snippets: The annotations were conducted using VivoQuant1 software.

questions.question: Which CNN model, DeepEdit or Full-CT, has a higher average accuracy in organ segmentation?
evidence_snippets: ...Full-CT, pre-trained on human CT scans, surpasses DeepEdit in organs sharing common labels...

questions.question: How many time points are captured in the CT scans referenced in the MICA dataset?
evidence_snippets: ...captured at three distinct time points: days 1, 3, and 7.

questions.question: What were the major limitations observed regarding organ segmentation from the DeepEdit model?
questions.gold_answer: Poor prediction for small volume organs and inconsistent segmentation masks.
evidence_snippets: ...prediction is extremely poor when an organ has a small physical volume...

questions.question: In the comparison of model predictions against manual measurements shown in Figure 5, which organ showed the highest correlation coefficient (R²) with Full-CT model predictions?
evidence_snippets: Notably, ... the Full-CT model does not generate zero values for predicted volumes. We also observe a robust correlation for the brain (R² = 0.77) ...

questions.question: What might be the impact of training models like Full-CT extensively on human CT scans compared to those like DeepEdit trained on limited NHP CT data?
questions.gold_answer: Models trained on human data might generalize better and predict more accurately due to diverse training data.
evidence_snippets: Our findings underscore the superiority of models trained on human data compared to those trained from scratch.
...the Full-CT model is trained on 1228 human CT scans...

questions.question: What technology is integrated with MONAI Label for model training and image analysis?
evidence_snippets: ...MONAI Label seamlessly integrates with the 3D Slicer platform...

questions.question: Between DeepEdit and Full-CT, which model consistently reported zero values for predicted organ volumes?
evidence_snippets: ...the DeepEdit model... predicts zero volumes for organs such as the right inguinal lymph nodes, spleen, bone marrow, and small/large intestines.

questions.question: Identify two significant recommendations for future work based on the discussions in this study.
questions.gold_answer: Expand training data diversity and explore active learning approaches for model annotation.
evidence_snippets: Given the diverse array of organs and their volumes, a crucial need for additional training data arises.
...we propose exploring an active learning approach instead of annotating each CT scan from scratch.
